mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and a rise in the federal funds rate indicates stagflationary pressure that squeezes corporate margins and triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening supply-side capacity and rising business investment, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and higher aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC remains negative, or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding the 0.4% monthly threshold in over 60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy vanishes when real yields are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply growth that boosts capital goods orders and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when HOUST and INDPRO diverge, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust economic expansion that sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in over 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of high market volatility.
